steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, $2a b -4$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• "$2a b -1$": Combining $-6 + 2$ gives $-4$, not $-1$. Eliminated.
  • "$2a b -4$": This matches $2ab - 4$ exactly. That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
  • "$2a b -5$": The constant would need to be $-5$, but it is $-4$. Eliminated.
  • "$2a b -8$": This would follow from $\left(2\right)(ab-3) - 2$, not $+2$. Eliminated.
